name: CS-8 Series VCF-02
description: Fully analog multimode filter with 121 selectable filter shapes built by combining five filter taps (ALL, BP6, LP12, BP18, LP24) with per-tap sign inversion. Unique Emph, 2nd Harm, and Drop parameters.

controls.name: Freq
controls.description: Cutoff / centre frequency. Knob range 2Hz to 35kHz; external CV extends to 40kHz at top and essentially no limit at bottom.

controls.name: Reso
controls.description: Resonance / Q factor from 0 (no peak) to self-oscillation. Onset depends on Emph setting (~Reso=6 when Emph=1, ~Reso=8 when Emph=0.3).

controls.name: Emph
controls.description: Level of emphasis / self-oscillation independent from resonance Q. Low values produce a whisper-quiet self-osc that sits in the mix; high values produce large self-osc levels.

controls.name: Drop
controls.description: Compensates the low-frequency gain loss that occurs when resonance is raised on LP filters. Only active in low-pass configurations; yellow on-LED confirms.
controls.detail: -12dB to 0dB · LP only · -3 to -6dB is a good compromise

controls.name: Gain
controls.description: Output gain trim between filter tap and output jack. Red clip LED warns of output stage clipping.
controls.detail: -20dB to 0dB · use to tame self-osc levels

controls.name: Input1
controls.description: Attenuator for audio input 1. Paired with a switch selecting 0dB or +20dB boost for the input preamp.

controls.name: Input2
controls.description: Attenuator for audio input 2. Paired with a phase-reverse switch (0° / 180°).

controls.name: CV1 / CV2
controls.description: Two scalable frequency CV inputs with adjustable attenuation and a 0°/180° phase invert switch each. Range per input: 0 to 3.6 oct/V.
controls.detail: 0 – 3.6 oct/V per input · phase invert per input

controls.name: CV-Res
controls.description: Attenuator and phase-invert for the Reso CV input. 0V-5V drives resonance from 0 to self-oscillation.

controls.name: 2nd Harm button
controls.description: Adds soft 2nd-harmonic (overtone) distortion post-filter. Enhances warmth, increases at higher resonance. Button inactive when CV is patched into 2nd H jack (LED still shows status).
controls.detail: On / Off · lights red when active · CV overrides button

controls.name: ALL / BP6 / LP12 / BP18 / LP24 buttons
controls.description: Five filter-tap selectors. Each button cycles off → green (+ added) → red (- subtracted) except ALL which is on/off only (blue). Combinations of the five produce 121 distinct filter shapes.
controls.detail: off / green(+) / red(-) · ALL = blue on/off · CV override per tap

inputs.name: Input1
inputs.description: AC-coupled audio input 1 with +20dB preamp option.

inputs.name: Input2
inputs.description: AC-coupled audio input 2 with phase reverse.

inputs.name: CV1
inputs.description: DC CV input for frequency modulation. Scaling set by CV1 knob (0 to 3.6 oct/V).

inputs.name: CV2
inputs.description: DC CV input for frequency modulation. Scaling set by CV2 knob.

inputs.name: 1 V/Oct
inputs.description: Calibrated 1V/octave input for pitch-tracking filter cutoff.

inputs.name: 0.5 V/Oct
inputs.description: Non-calibrated 0.5V/octave input for slower cutoff tracking.

inputs.name: Emph 0-5 V
inputs.description: CV input added to Emph knob value. Covers entire Emph range.

inputs.name: Reso 0-5 V
inputs.description: CV input for resonance. +5V with Reso=10 = maximum; -5V with Reso=10 drops Reso to 0.

inputs.name: Reso (CV)
inputs.description: DC CV routed through CV-Res attenuator and phase switch.

inputs.name: ALL
inputs.description: Gate-style CV override for ALL tap: ≥+3V = on, ≤+2V = off. Button disabled while patched.

inputs.name: BP6 / LP12 / BP18 / LP24
inputs.description: Tap-override CV inputs: ≥+3V = inverted, ≥+1V = non-inverted, <+1V = off. Sockets accept full audio range.

inputs.name: 2nd H
inputs.description: CV override for 2nd Harmonic button. ≥+3V on, ≤+2V off, hysteresis between.

outputs.name: Output
outputs.description: DC-coupled filtered audio output. Offset trimmable via rear P7 trimmer.
outputs.voltage: Clip LED at output stage limit

behaviors.name: 121 filter shapes by tap summing
behaviors.description: Each tap can be off, added (+, green), or subtracted (-, red). ALL is on/off. Sum of signs produces 27 low-pass, 18 high-pass, 13 band-pass, and 63 all-pass/notch/phaser shapes. Classic Moog-style 24dB LP is +LP24; high-pass is ALL on + LP12 red. The filter types chart in the manual lists labeled presets (T1-T9, B1-B5, H1-H5, A1-A21).

behaviors.name: Emph decoupled from Reso
behaviors.description: Unique to this filter: Emph sets the AMPLITUDE of self-oscillation, not the threshold. Low Emph = self-osc barely audible, high Emph = huge self-osc. Reso sets Q. Combination allows whispered singing resonance behind a dry signal — impossible on standard filters.

behaviors.name: Drop compensates LP bass loss
behaviors.trigger: turning Drop up on LP modes with resonance
behaviors.description: Recovers low-frequency gain that typical LP filters lose as resonance rises. -3 to -6dB is a good compromise between compensation and overload. Drop only operates in low-pass shapes (yellow on-LED indicates). Pushing Drop too hard saturates the filter input for optional overdrive.

behaviors.name: 2nd harmonic warmth
behaviors.description: Adds soft even-harmonic distortion that emphasizes 2nd harmonic (octave up). Effect deepens with resonance level. Produces a 'warm' coloration distinct from the ladder-style overdrive of classic filters.

behaviors.name: CV tap override
behaviors.trigger: cable patched into any tap CV jack (ALL, BP6, LP12, BP18, LP24, 2nd H)
behaviors.description: Patching disables the corresponding button. CV voltage determines state: <+1V off, +1V to +3V green (non-inverted), >+3V red (inverted). Allows sequenced filter-morph patches by stepping between tap states under CV.

behaviors.name: Dual CV inputs for FM
behaviors.trigger: patching CV1 and CV2 with knobs past 0
behaviors.description: Each of CV1/CV2 has its own attenuator and phase-invert. Scale 0 to 3.6 oct/V. Sockets are DC-coupled for audio-rate FM. Phase invert lets two correlated modulators partially cancel or reinforce. Combined with 1V/Oct and 0.5V/Oct sockets, the filter has five simultaneous frequency CV paths.

behaviors.name: Knob-programming caveat
behaviors.trigger: rapid real-time twisting of Freq or Reso
behaviors.description: Manual explicitly states these are programming controls, not performance potentiometers. Abusing them voids warranty coverage. Real-time sweeps should be done via CV — the module is designed to be sequencer / foot-pedal / external-controller driven.

behaviors.name: Clip LED at output
behaviors.description: Red clip LED on front panel shows output clipping — use Gain knob to back off, especially at high self-oscillation levels or with Drop pushed. Drive LED on the input side independently lights at 2Vpp to indicate overdrive onset.

patchIdeas.name: Classic 24dB ladder-style LP
patchIdeas.patch: LP24 green only · all others off · Reso ~6 Emph 0.5 · Drop -3dB to compensate bass loss · envelope into CV1 · sounds like a Moog ladder but with Schippmann transparency
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* LP24: green
* Reso: 6
* Emph: 0.5
* Drop: -3dB
* CV1: 1 oct/V

VCO:

Envelope:

VCA:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF-02 (CV1)
- VCF-02 (Output) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Super notch for formant shaping
patchIdeas.patch: Preset A6: ALL blue · LP24 red · BP18 green · LP12 green · BP6 red · deep 4-pole-style notch · modulate Freq for vocal-formant sweeps
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* ALL: blue on
* LP24: red
* BP18: green
* LP12: green
* BP6: red
* Reso: 4

VCO:

LFO:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- LFO (Tri) >> VCF-02 (CV1)
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Whisper self-oscillation behind mix
patchIdeas.patch: Emph 0.1 · Reso 10 · 1V/Oct from sequencer · self-oscillating sine sits quietly in the mix as a ghost melody under a dry lead · unique Emph/Reso split makes this possible
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* LP24: green
* Reso: 10 (self-osc)
* Emph: 0.1 (whisper)

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCF-02 (1 V/Oct)
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Two-stage phaser
patchIdeas.patch: Preset A9: ALL blue · LP24 green · BP18 red · LP12 red · BP6 red · audio rate LFO into CV2 with 180 invert · rich phaser tone
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* ALL: blue on
* LP24: green
* BP18: red
* LP12: red
* BP6: red
* Reso: 2
* CV2 phase: 180

VCO:

LFO:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- LFO (Tri) >> VCF-02 (CV2)
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: CV-morphed filter type
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer gate/CV into LP12 and LP24 jacks (overrides buttons) · different steps yield different filter shapes per step · the filter becomes a filter-type sequencer
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* ALL: blue on

Sequencer:

VCO:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- Sequencer (CV 1) >> VCF-02 (LP12) // steps through off/+/-
- Sequencer (CV 2) >> VCF-02 (LP24) // independent morph
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate resonance FM
patchIdeas.patch: LP24 green · Reso ~7 · VCO sine into Reso CV via CV-Res at ~50% · resonance wobbles at audio rate generating FM-like sidebands around the resonance peak
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* LP24: green
* Reso: 7
* CV-Res: ~50%

VCO A:

VCO B:

- VCO A (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- VCO B (Triangle) >> VCF-02 (Reso) // audio-rate resonance
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Warm 2nd-harm bass
patchIdeas.patch: LP12 green · LP24 green (both LP same sign = +6dB bass boost) · 2nd Harm on · Drop -6dB · saturated, warm, deep bass where the Schippmann's 2nd harmonic distortion sits perfectly
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* LP12: green
* LP24: green
* 2nd Harm: ON
* Drop: -6dB
* Reso: 5

VCO:

Envelope:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCF-02 (CV1)
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Phase-cancel dual CV sweep
patchIdeas.patch: CV1 knob ~2 oct/V · CV2 knob same · CV2 phase switch 180 · same modulator into both · the two opposing CVs partially cancel to yield a subtle filter nudge without full sweep range
patchIdeas.diagram:
VCF-02:
* CV1: 2 oct/V · phase 0
* CV2: 2 oct/V · phase 180

LFO:

VCO:

- VCO (Saw) -> VCF-02 (Input1)
- LFO (Tri) >> VCF-02 (CV1)
- LFO (Tri) >> VCF-02 (CV2) // phase-inverted, partial cancel
- VCF-02 (Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: HP from all-pass minus LP
patchIdeas.patch: ALL blue on · LP12 red · BP6 and BP18 off · clean 6dB HP preset H1 · resonance emphasizes the corner with no bass build-up · great for removing rumble on pads while keeping motion
